scenarioCoverage.scenario: Premerge finalization hashes clean candidate HEAD while retaining the controlled default-branch identity reference.
A real candidate commit survives a no-ff merge and passes detached post-merge preflight when canonical content is unchanged.
Included-content drift after the candidate merge fails closed.
Hosted release evidence resolves and records the exact merged default-branch SHA before tag mutation.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Fail closed on release identity, lifecycle, source-tree, archive, or ownership mismatch.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The drift topology test rejects changed included content; existing identity and lifecycle checks remain intact.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep candidate content evidence distinct from provider-bound published identity evidence.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The finalizer hashes candidate HEAD; the hosted workflow remains authoritative for exact merged-SHA release-source.json.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Use one Work Item, one branch, and one PR for the corrective process change.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: All changes are owned by release_postmerge_source_verification_v1 on codex/release-postmerge-source-verification.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not rewrite immutable archived Work Item evidence.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Historical plans link existing archive records; no archived Contract, Summary, or manifest was edited.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not publish or tag until post-merge verification is complete.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: No v0.5.39 tag or release mutation was performed in this Work Item.

risk.detail: Local topology and full-suite verification are green; hosted PR and post-merge CI remain required before release.

destructiveChanges: Deleted the superseded release plan only after recording its incorrect old-origin/main instruction and adding an evidence-backed retention rule; Git history remains the recovery path.
observedIssues: Premerge finalization resolved the controlled origin/main identity and therefore hashed the old base instead of the candidate content.
A prior correct candidate-tree implementation and its expectation had been reverted together, making local premerge verification self-consistently wrong.
The implementation plan referenced nonexistent make check-docs; it now uses make check-docs-metadata.
The plan-cleanup regression encoded a stale never-delete rule that prevented governed removal of a known-wrong plan.

residualRisks.detail: The exact merged SHA must still pass GitHub Actions and post-merge release preflight before v0.5.39 publication.

reviewReadiness.reason: All required verification passed; residual risk remains documented.
expectedReviewFocus: Confirm candidate HEAD is used only for premerge content materialization.
Confirm the release workflow remains the authority for exact merged-SHA identity and fails before tag mutation.
Confirm historical-plan compaction retains authoritative archive references.

intentAlignment.rationaleValidated: Candidate content and merged identity are now explicit separate stages; no metadata-only workaround or tag mutation was used.

boundaryChecks.runtimeEntrypoints: verified: existing finalizer and release-preflight entrypoints are unchanged
boundaryChecks.userVisibleOutput: verified: warning-only archive growth and fail-closed mismatch output are covered
boundaryChecks.persistence: verified: canonical release metadata semantics are documented and regression-tested
boundaryChecks.generatedArtifacts: verified: release-source.json remains hosted external exact-SHA evidence

userCorrectionsCaptured: Fix release process defects and stale code before retrying publication; do not apply temporary metadata workarounds.
Retain the current design and execution plan while resolving documentation budget through governed historical-plan cleanup.
Monitor GitHub Actions after CI starts.
userCorrectionSolidification: Real candidate-to-merge topology tests now protect the missing process transition.
Reference documentation separates candidate content from post-merge identity.
Plan retention policy and its regression permit evidence-backed compaction and deletion of known-wrong plans.
knownGaps: PR CI, merge, lifecycle closure, exact-main post-merge audit, and v0.5.39 publication are subsequent governed stages and have not yet occurred.
overclaimPrevention: Do not claim release completion until the corrective PR is merged and closed, exact main passes required CI and release preflight, and v0.5.39 tag/assets/evidence are verified.
